name: Russell Malone
abstract: Russell Lamar Malone (November 8, 1963 – August 23, 2024) was an American jazz guitarist. He began working with Jimmy Smith in 1988 and went on to work with Harry Connick Jr.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I don’t think I brought that, but you did – I think you did the record with – is that the record with Russell Malone on it? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Robert Glasper
about_quotes.evidence: As soon as I got in college I pretty much went on the road with Christian McBride and Russell Malone and Terence and a few other people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Robert Glasper interviewed by Greg Thomas, Clinton, New York, February 20, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: They used to come to my house... I always pass out what I consider my best advice, and those cats are doing well. So I’m proud of that.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kristen Korb
about_quotes.evidence: Russell Malone is really cool. I mean he took me to a bunch of jam sessions and we got a chance to do some playing
about_quotes.doc_title: Kristen Korb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I asked Russell Malone about it. He said, “Well, I came, I grew up in Florida.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: We were, Bob at Arcadia, Bob Carsey and I were talking about maybe doing a duet album at some point... I knew I wanted Russell, to do one with Russell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001
